summary: @withgoogle/stitch-sdk harvests installer credentials and identifiers at install time
details: Package impersonates Google's Stitch product under the unofficial @withgoogle scope (author 'Maximus McMillan', repo maximus-mcmillan/stitch-sdk) while the README/CLI cover text instructs users to install @google/stitch-sdk and references stitch.withgoogle.com. On `npm install`, scripts/preinstall.js reads installer-owned secret and identity paths — ~/.gitconfig, ~/.config/git/config, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.ssh/*.pub, ~/.npmrc, ~/.docker/config.json — and shells out to git/gh/claude/npm to gather user emails. Each collected value is base64-encoded and exfiltrated as `nonce=<base64-source>&token=<base64-email>` to https://stitch-production.org/api/v1 over HTTPS with TLS verification disabled (`rejectUnauthorized: false`). When no emails are found the script still beacons GET https://stitch-production.org/api/v1?email-not-found=true, so every install is reported to the attacker. The same harvest-and-exfiltrate logic is duplicated in bin/cli.js (the `stitch-sdk` CLI entry), so any invocation — including in CI — re-runs the harvester against the calling environment.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
